description: The entity identifies vulnerabilities through scanning or testing, prioritizes and tracks remediation, and performs scanning and patching on a defined cadence or through a defined workflow.

subcriteria.name: vulnerability_findings
subcriteria.reference_text: A dashboard, scan output, or security assessment shows actual vulnerability findings rather than only an inventory of software. Real evidence usually includes CVEs, severity labels such as critical or high, affected packages, images, hosts, or repositories, and often comes from Snyk, Dependabot, Trivy, Qualys, Nessus, or a penetration test report.

subcriteria.name: remediation_tracking_and_sla
subcriteria.reference_text: Evidence shows vulnerabilities are triaged and tracked through remediation rather than merely listed. Good evidence includes remediation status, owner, ticket, due date, SLA by severity, target fix version, accepted risk, retest result, or counts of resolved versus open critical findings.

subcriteria.name: scan_cadence_or_enforcement
subcriteria.reference_text: A policy, scheduled scan view, or CI/CD configuration shows vulnerability scanning happens on a defined cadence or continuously. Real evidence often says daily, weekly, monthly, scheduled scan, recurring scan, pull request check, pipeline gate, or remediation within 48 hours for critical findings.

subcriteria.name: penetration_test_or_security_assessment
subcriteria.reference_text: A penetration test or vulnerability assessment report identifies scope, test date, findings, and recommended remediation. Good evidence usually includes a remediation plan, management response, or follow-up status for discovered issues.

mismatch_signals.reason: A software inventory or dependency list without findings is not vulnerability management evidence by itself.

mismatch_signals.pattern: \bwe patch regularly\b|\bpatch as needed\b|\bpatches are applied regularly\b
mismatch_signals.reason: A vague statement about patching is not enough without cadence, SLAs, or tracked remediation.
Endpoint malware protection is related security evidence but not direct proof of vulnerability scanning and remediation.
